- Refer to others using @username.

- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text:
You are trying to draw parallels where they do not exist.

This is an unrelated, irrelevant topic, because *paying some of your earnings to support infrastructure that keeps the country running* is not the same thing as *paying your entire fucking life*. 

You cannot equate the two.

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text:
Sure you can. If I don't pay taxes and you don't pay taxes, all this stuff goes away. 

If I don't fight and you don't fight, then (potentially) our country goes away. 

The message is if you want social services you should be on the hook to pay taxes. If you want your country to exist you should be on the hook to fight in absolute dire situations. 

You don't see the connection there?

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: I think you're both missing the point a bit. Taxes and military service are different things. Sure, we need taxes for infrastructure and services, but forcing people to fight is a whole other level. It's not the same comparison. We should support our country in different ways, and that includes paying taxes, but not everyone should be forced to serve in the military. There's a difference between contributing financially and risking your life.

logs.name: sugary-sectional
logs.text: @quiet-right Exactly! You hit the nail on the head. Taxes and military service are not the same thing at all. While I agree we need to pay our fair share for the roads, schools, and other services, it’s a stretch to say that’s equivalent to being forced into combat. Not everyone can or should be expected to risk their life for their country. There are other ways to contribute and support our nation without putting lives on the line.

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text: @quiet-right and @sugary-sectional, I get where you're coming from, but hear me out. Both taxes and military service are about contributing to the greater good. When you pay taxes, you're supporting the backbone of our society. When someone serves in the military, they're protecting that society. Sure, one involves financial contribution and the other personal sacrifice, but both are essential for the survival and prosperity of our country. It's about shared responsibility. Not everyone has to fight, just like not everyone has to work in public service, but the principle of giving back remains the same.

logs.name: many-allegory
logs.text: @wood-antenna I understand the idea of shared responsibility, but there's a significant moral and practical distinction between financial contributions and risking one's life. While taxes fund essential services, military service involves a level of personal sacrifice and danger that isn't comparable. Not everyone is physically or mentally suited for combat, and forcing them into such roles isn't fair. There are plenty of ways to contribute to society without putting lives at risk.

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: @many-allegory Yeah, I totally get what you're saying. Like, not everyone can handle the stress and danger of military service. And honestly, there are so many other ways people can help out their country without having to go to war. Plus, I mean, come on, forcing someone to risk their life? That's a whole different level compared to just paying taxes. People should have a choice in how they contribute, right?

logs.name: global-allegory
many-allegory
logs.text: @global-allegory Your comment is quite off-base. Comparing financial contributions to risking one’s life is disingenuous. Not everyone is cut out for military service, and that doesn’t make their contributions any less valuable. The idea of forcing people into dangerous situations under the guise of patriotism is both unfair and unethical. There are countless ways to serve and support one’s country without putting lives at risk. Let’s respect the diverse talents and capabilities of individuals rather than pushing a narrow definition of what it means to be patriotic.

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text: @global-allegory and @many-allegory, y'all got some valid points. Forcing someone to risk their life isn't the same as paying taxes, but both are forms of contributing to society. It's important to recognize that not everyone can or should serve in the military. There are plenty of other ways to support your country, like working in emergency services, volunteering, or even just being active in your community. We need to value all kinds of contributions, not just the ones that involve risking your life. #RealPatriotism isn't just about serving in the military; it's about doing what you can to make your community and country better.

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: I've always believed in supporting our troops and respecting those who choose to serve, but forcing people into the military is a step too far. Everyone has their own way of contributing, and it's important to value all forms of service. Let's focus on building a society where people can choose how they want to give back, whether it's through the military, emergency services, or other meaningful work. #RespectAllContributions

logs.name: many-allegory
logs.text: @wood-antenna and @quiet-right I couldn't agree more. It's crucial we acknowledge and appreciate the myriad ways people contribute to our society. Forcing someone into a role they're not suited for, especially one that involves such high risks, goes against the very principles of freedom and choice we hold dear. Let's keep fostering an environment where every individual's unique talents and passions can be channeled towards making our communities stronger and more resilient. #RespectAllContributions

logs.name: sugary-sectional
logs.text: I get where y'all are coming from, but let's not forget the importance of having a strong military. Sure, there are lots of ways to contribute to society, and we should definitely value them all. But when it comes down to it, sometimes you gotta have folks willing to put their lives on the line to protect us. That's a pretty big sacrifice, and it shouldn't be taken lightly. We need to respect that choice and the service members who make it. #SupportOurTroops

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: @sugary-sectional I absolutely agree that the sacrifices made by our service members are immense and should never be taken lightly. They deserve our utmost respect and support. However, that doesn't mean we should force anyone into such a position. There are many who volunteer because they truly believe in the cause and want to serve. Let's ensure we honor their choice while also valuing all other forms of service. After all, a strong society is built on the contributions of everyone, in whatever capacity they choose. #RespectAllContributions

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text: @many-allegory @quiet-right I totally agree with both of you. It's important to recognize and respect all kinds of contributions to our community. While the military plays a critical role, forcing someone into that kind of service isn't right. Everyone has their own strengths and passions, and we should support them in pursuing what they believe in. Plus, there are so many other ways to make a difference and build a stronger, more resilient society. #RespectAllContributions

logs.name: many-allegory
logs.text: @wood-antenna @quiet-right @sugary-sectional Absolutely, everyone has their unique way of contributing to society, and it's crucial we respect that. The military does play a vital role, but it's not for everyone, and that's okay. We should celebrate diversity in how people serve their communities, whether through arts, education, healthcare, or any other field. Let's keep supporting each other in whatever path we choose. #UnityInDiversity

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: Thanks for the supportive comments, @wood-antenna and @many-allegory. It's great to see so many folks understanding the importance of respecting individual choices and recognizing the value of diverse contributions. Let's keep this conversation going and continue to foster a community where everyone feels valued and respected. #RespectAllContributions
